triage.explanation: Fast route by default; no hard factual risk trigger was found.

problem: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit must detect tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
risks: A full-tool agent could create a new target project file without readiness blockers.

verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary snapshots covering git tracked/untracked files and fallback walks, excluding only the dispatch artifact root.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() uses git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z to capture tracked, cached, and untracked files while respecting .gitignore.
evidence.why: This covers tracked (-c) and other/untracked (-o) files in git-backed projects.

evidence.summary: walkFiles() recursively walks directories excluding node_modules, .git, dist, coverage, .next, .cache as fallback for non-git projects.
evidence.why: This provides filesystem walk fallback when git is not available.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() creates Map of file->sha256 digests, excluding allowedRoots (dispatch artifact root). Uses gitFiles() with walkFiles() fallback.
evidence.why: This is the core boundary snapshot implementation covering all project files except allowed artifacts.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after snapshot Maps and returns added/modified/deleted changes.
evidence.why: This detects mutations by comparing digest changes.

evidence.summary: Runtime captures boundaryBefore/after snapshots and runs diff, with allowedRoots excluding dispatch artifact root.
evidence.why: This shows the boundary audit is integrated into the dispatch runtime.

evidence.summary: Test verifies git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations by writing untracked-mutation.txt and expecting BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker.
evidence.why: This is explicit evidence that the system is tested for untracked file detection.

evidence.summary: Test verifies boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass, using non-git temp directory.
evidence.why: This tests the non-git walk fallback for mutation detection.

evidence.summary: Doctrine states dispatch runs boundary audit before/after and any mutation outside artifact root becomes a readiness blocker.
evidence.why: This documents the intended boundary audit contract.

risks: git ls-files behavior may differ across git versions or non-standard git configurations
SHA256 digest collision risk is theoretical but not practical
missing: Real-world stress-test evidence with actual Claude -p agents against live projects (the demand states 'must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents' but only mock agents are tested)
Non-git project tests are in temp directories; evidence for existing non-git projects is missing
Evidence that boundary snapshots correctly exclude nested .yolo/demand/evidence/dispatch-* directories when they exist

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary snapshots covering git tracked/untracked files and fallback walks, excluding only the dispatch artifact root.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() uses git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z to capture tracked, cached, and untracked files while respecting .gitignore.
evidence.why: Verifies coverage of tracked (-c) and untracked (-o) files in git-backed projects.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() creates Map of file->sha256 digests, excluding allowedRoots (dispatch artifact root).
evidence.why: Verifies core boundary snapshot excludes dispatch artifact root.

evidence.summary: Test verifies git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations.
evidence.why: Confirms untracked file detection is tested.

evidence.summary: Test verifies boundary blocks project file mutation in non-git temp directory.
evidence.why: Confirms walk fallback is tested for mutation detection.

evidence.summary: Doctrine states dispatch runs boundary audit before/after and any mutation outside artifact root becomes a readiness blocker.

risks: No test coverage for nested .yolo/demand/evidence/dispatch-* directory exclusion scenarios
Non-git walk tests only use temp directories, not existing non-git projects
Implementation exists but has not been stress-tested with real Claude -p agents as required by status_quo

result.notes: Evidence paths are valid, sources are properly scoped, and explorer correctly separated facts from missing data. The missing items (real-world stress tests, nested directory exclusion coverage, existing non-git project tests) are documented as gaps but not blockers to PRD readiness—the implementation exists and is unit-tested. The status_quo acknowledgment of required stress-testing means this is a known requirement, not an assumption promoted as fact.
